de.question: Wer gewann 2018 in Russland?
de.explanation: Frankreich besiegte Kroatien im Finale mit 4:2 und holte sich den zweiten Titel.

es.question: ¿Quién ganó en 2018, en Rusia?
es.explanation: Francia venció a Croacia 4–2 en la final y consiguió su segundo título.

es-ES.question: ¿Quién ganó en 2018, en Rusia?
es-ES.explanation: Francia derrotó a Croacia por 4–2 en la final y logró su segundo título.

fr.question: Qui a gagné en 2018, en Russie ?
fr.explanation: La France a battu la Croatie 4–2 en finale pour décrocher son deuxième titre.

it.question: Chi ha vinto nel 2018, in Russia?
it.explanation: La Francia ha battuto la Croazia 4–2 in finale conquistando il suo secondo titolo.

ja.question: 2018年、ロシアで優勝したのはどこ？
ja.explanation: フランスは決勝でクロアチアを4対2で破り、2度目の優勝を果たしました。

ko.question: 2018년 러시아에서 우승한 팀은?
ko.explanation: 프랑스가 결승에서 크로아티아를 4-2로 꺾고 두 번째 우승을 차지했습니다.

pt-BR.question: Quem venceu em 2018, na Rússia?
pt-BR.explanation: A França venceu a Croácia por 4 a 2 na final e conquistou seu segundo título.

zh-Hans.question: 2018年在俄罗斯夺冠的是哪支球队？
zh-Hans.explanation: 法国在决赛中以4比2击败克罗地亚，赢得了队史第二个冠军。

questions.question: Who was sent off in the 2006 final for headbutting Marco Materazzi?
questions.explanation: Zidane’s headbutt on Materazzi in extra time of the 2006 final was his last act as a professional — Italy won on penalties. Zidane played for Cannes, Bordeaux, Juventus, and Real Madrid, where he scored one of the greatest Champions League final goals ever (a left-foot volley in 2002). He later said he didn’t regret the headbutt itself because Materazzi’s insults about his mother and sister were unacceptable, though he regretted that millions of children saw it.

ar.question: من طُرد في نهائي 2006 بسبب نطحه ماركو ماتيراتزي؟
ar.explanation: كانت نطحة زيدان لماتيراتزي في الوقت الإضافي من نهائي 2006 آخر تصرف له كلاعب محترف — وفازت إيطاليا بركلات الترجيح. لعب زيدان لكان وبوردو ويوفنتوس وريال مدريد، حيث سجل أحد أروع أهداف النهائيات في تاريخ دوري الأبطال (تسديدة بالقدم اليسرى من الطائرة عام 2002). قال لاحقًا إنه لم يندم على النطحة نفسها لأن إهانات ماتيراتزي بحق أمه وأخته كانت غير مقبولة، لكنه ندم على أن ملايين الأطفال شاهدوا ذلك.

de.question: Wer wurde im Finale 2006 nach einem Kopfstoß gegen Marco Materazzi des Feldes verwiesen?
de.explanation: Zidanes Kopfstoß gegen Materazzi in der Verlängerung des Finales 2006 war seine letzte Aktion als Profi — Italien gewann im Elfmeterschießen. Zidane spielte für Cannes, Bordeaux, Juventus und Real Madrid, wo er eines der schönsten Finaltore der Champions-League-Geschichte erzielte (ein Volleyschuss mit dem linken Fuß im Jahr 2002). Später sagte er, dass er den Kopfstoß selbst nicht bereue, weil Materazzis Beleidigungen gegen seine Mutter und Schwester inakzeptabel gewesen seien, er es aber bedauere, dass Millionen Kinder es gesehen hätten.

es.question: ¿Quién fue expulsado en la final de 2006 por darle un cabezazo a Marco Materazzi?
es.explanation: El cabezazo de Zidane a Materazzi en el tiempo extra de la final de 2006 fue su último acto como profesional: Italia ganó en los penales. Zidane jugó en el Cannes, el Burdeos, la Juventus y el Real Madrid, donde marcó uno de los mejores goles en la historia de las finales de la Champions League (una volea de zurda en 2002). Más tarde dijo que no se arrepentía del cabezazo en sí, porque los insultos de Materazzi sobre su madre y su hermana eran inaceptables, aunque sí lamentaba que millones de niños lo hubieran visto.

es-ES.question: ¿A quién expulsaron en la final de 2006 por dar un cabezazo a Marco Materazzi?
es-ES.explanation: El cabezazo de Zidane a Materazzi en la prórroga de la final de 2006 fue su última acción como profesional: Italia ganó en los penaltis. Zidane jugó en el Cannes, el Burdeos, la Juventus y el Real Madrid, donde marcó uno de los mejores goles de la historia de las finales de la Champions League (una volea de zurda en 2002). Más tarde dijo que no se arrepentía del cabezazo en sí, porque los insultos de Materazzi sobre su madre y su hermana fueron inaceptables, aunque sí lamentaba que millones de niños lo hubieran visto.

fr.question: Qui a été expulsé lors de la finale 2006 pour avoir donné un coup de tête à Marco Materazzi ?
fr.explanation: Le coup de tête de Zidane sur Materazzi dans la prolongation de la finale 2006 fut son dernier geste en tant que professionnel — l'Italie l'a emporté aux tirs au but. Zidane a évolué à Cannes, Bordeaux, la Juventus et au Real Madrid, où il a inscrit l'un des plus beaux buts de l'histoire des finales de Ligue des champions (une reprise de volée du pied gauche en 2002). Il a déclaré plus tard qu'il ne regrettait pas le coup de tête lui-même, car les insultes de Materazzi envers sa mère et sa sœur étaient inacceptables, mais qu'il regrettait que des millions d'enfants l'aient vu.

it.question: Chi fu espulso nella finale del 2006 per aver dato una testata a Marco Materazzi?
it.explanation: La testata di Zidane a Materazzi nei tempi supplementari della finale del 2006 fu il suo ultimo gesto da professionista: l'Italia vinse ai rigori. Zidane ha giocato nel Cannes, nel Bordeaux, nella Juventus e nel Real Madrid, dove segnò uno dei gol più belli nella storia delle finali di Champions League (una volée di sinistro nel 2002). In seguito disse di non pentirsi della testata in sé, perché gli insulti di Materazzi verso sua madre e sua sorella erano inaccettabili, pur rammaricandosi che milioni di bambini l'avessero vista.

ja.question: 2006年の決勝でマルコ・マテラッツィに頭突きをして退場になったのは誰？
options: パトリック・ヴィエラ
ジネディーヌ・ジダン
ja.explanation: 2006年決勝の延長戦でマテラッツィに放ったジダンの頭突きは、プロとしての最後のプレーとなり、試合はイタリアがPK戦で勝利した。ジダンはカンヌ、ボルドー、ユヴェントス、レアル・マドリードでプレーし、レアルでは2002年のチャンピオンズリーグ決勝で左足のボレーという史上屈指の名ゴールを決めた。後に彼は、マテラッツィが母と姉妹を侮辱したことは許せないことだったので頭突きそのものは後悔していないが、数百万人の子どもたちがそれを見たことは悔やまれると語った。

ko.question: 2006년 결승전에서 마르코 마테라치에게 박치기를 해 퇴장당한 선수는?
ko.explanation: 2006년 결승 연장전에서 마테라치에게 가한 지단의 박치기는 그의 프로 선수로서 마지막 행동이었고, 경기는 이탈리아가 승부차기로 승리했다. 지단은 칸, 보르도, 유벤투스, 레알 마드리드에서 뛰었으며, 레알에서는 2002년 챔피언스리그 결승에서 왼발 발리슛이라는 역대 최고의 결승골 중 하나를 터뜨렸다. 그는 훗날 마테라치가 자신의 어머니와 누이를 모욕한 것은 용납할 수 없는 일이었기에 박치기 자체는 후회하지 않지만, 수백만 명의 아이들이 그 장면을 본 것은 후회한다고 말했다.

pt-BR.question: Quem foi expulso na final de 2006 por dar uma cabeçada em Marco Materazzi?
pt-BR.explanation: A cabeçada de Zidane em Materazzi na prorrogação da final de 2006 foi o seu último ato como profissional — a Itália venceu nos pênaltis. Zidane jogou pelo Cannes, Bordeaux, Juventus e Real Madrid, onde marcou um dos gols mais bonitos da história das finais da Champions League (uma bicicleta de pé esquerdo em 2002). Mais tarde, ele disse que não se arrependia da cabeçada em si, pois os insultos de Materazzi à sua mãe e irmã foram inaceitáveis, embora lamentasse que milhões de crianças tivessem visto aquilo.

zh-Hans.question: 在2006年决赛中，谁因头顶马尔科·马特拉齐而被罚下场？
zh-Hans.explanation: 齐达内在2006年决赛加时赛中头顶马特拉齐，这成了他职业生涯的最后一个动作——意大利最终通过点球大战获胜。齐达内曾效力于戛纳、波尔多、尤文图斯和皇家马德里，并在皇马打入了冠军联赛决赛史上最精彩的进球之一（2002年的一记左脚凌空抽射）。他后来表示并不后悔那记头顶本身，因为马特拉齐对他母亲和姐妹的侮辱令人无法接受，但他遗憾的是有数百万儿童目睹了这一幕。

questions.question: Lumen Field, the 2026 venue in Seattle, is home to which MLS team?
questions.explanation: Lumen Field is home to the Seattle Sounders and is famous for its electric atmosphere.

ar.question: ملعب لومن فيلد، أحد ملاعب بطولة فوتبول 2026 في سياتل، هو معقل أي فريق من فرق الدوري الأمريكي للمحترفين (MLS)؟
ar.explanation: ملعب لومن فيلد هو معقل فريق سياتل ساوندرز، ويشتهر بأجوائه المتحمسة والمفعمة بالحماس.

de.question: Das Lumen Field, eine der Spielstätten von Football 2026 in Seattle, ist die Heimat welches MLS-Teams?
de.explanation: Das Lumen Field ist die Heimat der Seattle Sounders und berühmt für seine elektrisierende Atmosphäre.

es.question: El Lumen Field, una de las sedes de Football 2026 en Seattle, ¿es el estadio de qué equipo de la MLS?
es.explanation: El Lumen Field es la casa del Seattle Sounders y es famoso por su ambiente vibrante.

es-ES.question: El Lumen Field, una de las sedes de Football 2026 en Seattle, ¿es el estadio de qué equipo de la MLS?
es-ES.explanation: El Lumen Field es el feudo del Seattle Sounders y es famoso por su ambiente electrizante.

fr.question: Le Lumen Field, l'un des stades de Football 2026 à Seattle, est l'enceinte de quelle équipe de MLS ?
fr.explanation: Le Lumen Field est le stade des Seattle Sounders et est réputé pour son ambiance électrique.

it.question: Il Lumen Field, una delle sedi di Football 2026 a Seattle, è lo stadio di quale squadra della MLS?
it.explanation: Il Lumen Field è la casa dei Seattle Sounders ed è famoso per la sua atmosfera elettrizzante.

ja.question: 2026年のシアトル会場であるルーメン・フィールドは、どのMLSチームの本拠地でしょう？
options: ポートランド・ティンバーズ
シアトル・サウンダーズ
バンクーバー・ホワイトキャップス
ja.explanation: ルーメン・フィールドはシアトル・サウンダーズの本拠地で、熱気あふれる雰囲気で知られています。

ko.question: 2026년 시애틀 경기장인 루멘 필드는 어느 MLS 팀의 홈구장일까요?
ko.explanation: 루멘 필드는 시애틀 사운더스의 홈구장으로, 짜릿한 분위기로 유명합니다.

pt-BR.question: O Lumen Field, uma das sedes do Football 2026 em Seattle, é a casa de qual time da MLS?
pt-BR.explanation: O Lumen Field é a casa do Seattle Sounders e é famoso por sua atmosfera eletrizante.

zh-Hans.question: 卢门球场（Lumen Field）是2026年西雅图的比赛场地，它是哪支MLS球队的主场？
zh-Hans.explanation: 卢门球场是西雅图音浪队的主场，以其热烈火爆的现场氛围而闻名。

questions.question: How many titles has France won?
questions.explanation: France has won twice — at home in 1998 and in Russia in 2018. Winning as hosts is not unique: Uruguay (1930), Italy (1934), England (1966), West Germany (1974), and Argentina (1978) all won on home soil. However, several hosts have famously failed — including Brazil, who lost the 1950 final to Uruguay at the Maracanã in one of football’s greatest upsets.

ar.question: كم عدد الألقاب التي فازت بها فرنسا؟
ar.explanation: فازت فرنسا مرتين — على أرضها عام 1998 وفي روسيا عام 2018. والفوز على الأرض ليس أمرًا فريدًا: فقد توّجت أوروغواي (1930) وإيطاليا (1934) وإنجلترا (1966) وألمانيا الغربية (1974) والأرجنتين (1978) جميعها على ملاعبها. ومع ذلك، أخفق عدد من المضيفين بشكل مشهور — ومنهم البرازيل التي خسرت نهائي 1950 أمام أوروغواي على ملعب ماراكانا في واحدة من أكبر المفاجآت في تاريخ كرة القدم.

de.question: Wie viele Titel hat Frankreich gewonnen?
de.explanation: Frankreich hat zweimal gewonnen — 1998 im eigenen Land und 2018 in Russland. Ein Sieg als Gastgeber ist nichts Einzigartiges: Uruguay (1930), Italien (1934), England (1966), Westdeutschland (1974) und Argentinien (1978) triumphierten allesamt auf heimischem Boden. Mehrere Gastgeber sind jedoch berühmt gescheitert — darunter Brasilien, das 1950 im Maracanã das Finale gegen Uruguay verlor, in einer der größten Sensationen der Fußballgeschichte.

es.question: ¿Cuántos títulos ha ganado Francia?
es.explanation: Francia ha ganado dos veces: en casa en 1998 y en Rusia en 2018. Ganar como anfitrión no es algo exclusivo: Uruguay (1930), Italia (1934), Inglaterra (1966), Alemania Occidental (1974) y Argentina (1978) se coronaron en su propio suelo. Sin embargo, varios anfitriones fracasaron de forma célebre, entre ellos Brasil, que perdió la final de 1950 ante Uruguay en el Maracaná en una de las mayores sorpresas en la historia del fútbol.

es-ES.question: ¿Cuántos títulos ha ganado Francia?
es-ES.explanation: Francia ha ganado dos veces: en casa en 1998 y en Rusia en 2018. Ganar como anfitrión no es algo único: Uruguay (1930), Italia (1934), Inglaterra (1966), Alemania Occidental (1974) y Argentina (1978) se coronaron en su propio suelo. Sin embargo, varios anfitriones fracasaron de forma sonada, entre ellos Brasil, que perdió la final de 1950 ante Uruguay en el Maracaná en una de las mayores sorpresas de la historia del fútbol.

fr.question: Combien de titres la France a-t-elle remportés ?
fr.explanation: La France a triomphé à deux reprises — à domicile en 1998 et en Russie en 2018. Gagner en tant que pays hôte n'a rien d'unique : l'Uruguay (1930), l'Italie (1934), l'Angleterre (1966), l'Allemagne de l'Ouest (1974) et l'Argentine (1978) se sont tous imposés sur leurs terres. Plusieurs hôtes ont toutefois connu des échecs retentissants — notamment le Brésil, battu en finale en 1950 par l'Uruguay au Maracanã, lors de l'une des plus grandes surprises de l'histoire du football.

it.question: Quanti titoli ha vinto la Francia?
it.explanation: La Francia ha trionfato due volte: in casa nel 1998 e in Russia nel 2018. Vincere da padroni di casa non è una rarità: Uruguay (1930), Italia (1934), Inghilterra (1966), Germania Ovest (1974) e Argentina (1978) si sono tutte imposte sul proprio suolo. Tuttavia, diversi paesi ospitanti hanno clamorosamente fallito — tra cui il Brasile, sconfitto nella finale del 1950 dall'Uruguay al Maracanã in una delle più grandi sorprese nella storia del calcio.

ja.question: フランスは何回優勝していますか？
ja.explanation: フランスは2度優勝しています——1998年に自国開催で、そして2018年にロシアで優勝しました。開催国としての優勝は珍しいことではなく、ウルグアイ（1930年）、イタリア（1934年）、イングランド（1966年）、西ドイツ（1974年）、アルゼンチン（1978年）はいずれも自国で頂点に立ちました。しかし、いくつかの開催国は有名な失敗を喫しており、その一つがブラジルです。1950年の決勝でマラカナンにてウルグアイに敗れ、サッカー史上最大級の番狂わせとなりました。

ko.question: 프랑스는 몇 번 우승했나요?
ko.explanation: 프랑스는 두 번 우승했습니다 — 1998년 자국에서, 그리고 2018년 러시아에서 우승했습니다. 개최국으로서의 우승은 드문 일이 아닙니다. 우루과이(1930년), 이탈리아(1934년), 잉글랜드(1966년), 서독(1974년), 아르헨티나(1978년)가 모두 자국에서 정상에 올랐습니다. 그러나 여러 개최국이 유명한 실패를 맛보기도 했습니다 — 그중 브라질은 1950년 결승에서 마라카낭에서 우루과이에 패하며 축구 역사상 가장 큰 이변 중 하나를 남겼습니다.

pt-BR.question: Quantos títulos a França conquistou?
pt-BR.explanation: A França venceu duas vezes — em casa, em 1998, e na Rússia, em 2018. Vencer como anfitrião não é algo exclusivo: Uruguai (1930), Itália (1934), Inglaterra (1966), Alemanha Ocidental (1974) e Argentina (1978) todos triunfaram em seu próprio território. No entanto, vários anfitriões falharam de forma marcante — incluindo o Brasil, que perdeu a final de 1950 para o Uruguai no Maracanã, em uma das maiores zebras da história do futebol.

zh-Hans.question: 法国队夺得过多少次冠军？
zh-Hans.explanation: 法国队两次夺冠——1998年在本土，以及2018年在俄罗斯。东道主夺冠并不罕见：乌拉圭（1930年）、意大利（1934年）、英格兰（1966年）、西德（1974年）和阿根廷（1978年）都曾在本土登顶。然而，也有几支东道主球队遭遇了著名的失利——其中包括巴西，他们在1950年决赛中于马拉卡纳球场负于乌拉圭，成为足球史上最大的冷门之一。

questions.question: Spain's 2010 victory was built on a possession-based style known as…
questions.explanation: Spain's short-passing, possession-heavy 'tiki-taka' style dominated the 2010 tournament, crowned by Iniesta's extra-time winner.

questions.question: In which year did Canada make their tournament debut?
questions.explanation: Canada qualified for the 1986 tournament in Mexico, losing all three group matches. They returned 36 years later in 2022.

questions.question: Whose 'Hand of God' goal came in 1986?
questions.explanation: Diego Maradona punched in a goal against England, then scored the 'Goal of the Century' minutes later.

questions.question: SoFi Stadium, a 2026 venue in Los Angeles, is home to which two NFL teams?
questions.explanation: SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California is home to both the LA Rams and LA Chargers.

questions.question: Who scored the fastest goal ever — just 11 seconds into a 2002 match?
questions.explanation: Türkiye's Hakan Şükür scored after 11 seconds against South Korea in the 2002 third-place match.

questions.question: How many points does a team get for a win in the group stage?
questions.explanation: A win is worth 3 points, a draw 1, and a loss 0.

questions.question: A player is offside if, when the ball is passed to them, they are…
options: Ahead of the second-to-last defender and closer to the goal
questions.explanation: A player is offside if any part of their body (except hands and arms) is closer to the goal line than both the ball and the second-to-last defender.

questions.question: If two teams finish level on points in a group, what's the first tiebreaker?
questions.explanation: Goal difference across all group matches is checked first, then goals scored, then head-to-head.

questions.question: Who won the Golden Ball (best player) at the 2022 tournament?
questions.explanation: Messi won the Golden Ball with 7 goals and 3 assists, leading Argentina to the title.

questions.question: When a player is shown a red card, the team may…
options: Play a player short for the rest of the match
questions.explanation: A sent-off player cannot be replaced — the team plays a player down, and the player is suspended for at least the next match.

questions.question: If a knockout match is level after 90 minutes, what happens next?
questions.explanation: From the Round of 32 on, a tie goes to two 15-minute periods of extra time, then a penalty shootout if still level.

questions.question: Which historic stadium hosts the opening match in 2026?
questions.explanation: Mexico City's Estadio Azteca opens the tournament — a venue that has hosted two previous finals.

questions.question: Who won the tournament as captain in 1998 and as head coach in 2018?
questions.explanation: Didier Deschamps lifted the trophy as France's captain in 1998 and guided them to the title as coach 20 years later.

ar.question: من فاز بالبطولة كقائد عام 1998 وكمدرب رئيسي عام 2018؟
ar.explanation: رفع ديدييه ديشان الكأس كقائد لمنتخب فرنسا عام 1998، ثم قاده إلى اللقب كمدرب بعد 20 عامًا.

de.question: Wer gewann das Turnier 1998 als Kapitän und 2018 als Cheftrainer?
de.explanation: Didier Deschamps stemmte 1998 als Kapitän Frankreichs den Pokal in die Höhe und führte die Mannschaft 20 Jahre später als Trainer zum Titel.

es.question: ¿Quién ganó el torneo como capitán en 1998 y como director técnico en 2018?
es.explanation: Didier Deschamps levantó el trofeo como capitán de Francia en 1998 y los llevó al título como entrenador 20 años después.

es-ES.question: ¿Quién ganó el torneo como capitán en 1998 y como seleccionador en 2018?
es-ES.explanation: Didier Deschamps levantó el trofeo como capitán de Francia en 1998 y la condujo al título como entrenador 20 años después.

fr.question: Qui a remporté le tournoi en tant que capitaine en 1998 et en tant que sélectionneur en 2018 ?
fr.explanation: Didier Deschamps a soulevé le trophée comme capitaine de la France en 1998, puis l'a menée au titre comme entraîneur 20 ans plus tard.

it.question: Chi ha vinto il torneo da capitano nel 1998 e da commissario tecnico nel 2018?
it.explanation: Didier Deschamps ha alzato il trofeo da capitano della Francia nel 1998 e l'ha guidata al titolo da allenatore 20 anni dopo.

ja.question: 1998年にキャプテンとして、2018年に監督としてこの大会を制したのは誰でしょう？
options: ジネディーヌ・ジダン
ディディエ・デシャン
ja.explanation: ディディエ・デシャンは1998年にフランスのキャプテンとしてトロフィーを掲げ、その20年後には監督としてチームを優勝に導きました。

ko.question: 1998년에는 주장으로, 2018년에는 감독으로 이 대회에서 우승한 인물은 누구일까요?
ko.explanation: 디디에 데샹은 1998년 프랑스 주장으로 트로피를 들어 올렸고, 20년 뒤에는 감독으로 팀을 우승으로 이끌었습니다.

pt-BR.question: Quem venceu o torneio como capitão em 1998 e como treinador em 2018?
pt-BR.explanation: Didier Deschamps ergueu o troféu como capitão da França em 1998 e a levou ao título como técnico 20 anos depois.

zh-Hans.question: 谁曾在1998年以队长身份、又在2018年以主教练身份赢得这项赛事的冠军？
zh-Hans.explanation: 迪迪埃·德尚在1998年作为法国队长高举奖杯，20年后又以主教练身份带领球队夺冠。

questions.question: Which team was the last to win two consecutive titles?
options: Italy, spearheaded by Meazza and Piola
Brazil, with Pelé and Garrincha in their prime
questions.explanation: Brazil's back-to-back wins in 1958 and 1962 remain the last time any nation successfully defended the title.

ar.question: أي منتخب كان آخر من فاز بلقبين متتاليين؟
options: البرازيل مع بيليه وغارينشا في أوج عطائهما
ar.explanation: يظل فوز البرازيل المتتالي في عامي 1958 و1962 آخر مرة ينجح فيها منتخب في الدفاع عن لقبه.

de.question: Welche Mannschaft gewann zuletzt zwei aufeinanderfolgende Titel?
options: Italien, angeführt von Meazza und Piola
Brasilien, mit Pelé und Garrincha in Bestform
de.explanation: Brasiliens aufeinanderfolgende Siege 1958 und 1962 bleiben das letzte Mal, dass eine Nation den Titel erfolgreich verteidigte.

es.question: ¿Qué selección fue la última en ganar dos títulos consecutivos?
options: Italia, liderada por Meazza y Piola
Brasil, con Pelé y Garrincha en su mejor momento
Argentina, bajo la genialidad de Maradona
Alemania, sostenida por la defensa de Beckenbauer
es.explanation: Los triunfos consecutivos de Brasil en 1958 y 1962 siguen siendo la última vez que una selección logró defender el título.

es-ES.question: ¿Qué selección fue la última en ganar dos títulos consecutivos?
options: Italia, encabezada por Meazza y Piola
Brasil, con Pelé y Garrincha en su mejor momento
Argentina, bajo la genialidad de Maradona
Alemania, apoyada en la defensa de Beckenbauer
es-ES.explanation: Las victorias consecutivas de Brasil en 1958 y 1962 siguen siendo la última vez que una selección logró defender el título.

fr.question: Quelle équipe a été la dernière à remporter deux titres consécutifs ?
options: L'Italie, emmenée par Meazza et Piola
Le Brésil, avec Pelé et Garrincha à leur apogée
L'Argentine, portée par le génie de Maradona
L'Allemagne, appuyée sur la défense de Beckenbauer
fr.explanation: Les victoires consécutives du Brésil en 1958 et 1962 restent la dernière fois qu'une nation a su défendre son titre avec succès.

it.question: Quale squadra è stata l'ultima a vincere due titoli consecutivi?
options: L'Italia, trascinata da Meazza e Piola
Il Brasile, con Pelé e Garrincha al massimo della forma
L'Argentina, sotto il genio di Maradona
La Germania, fondata sulla difesa di Beckenbauer
it.explanation: Le vittorie consecutive del Brasile nel 1958 e nel 1962 restano l'ultima volta in cui una nazionale è riuscita a difendere il titolo.

ja.question: 2大会連続で優勝した最後のチームはどこですか？
options: メアッツァとピオラが率いたイタリア
全盛期のペレとガリンシャを擁したブラジル
マラドーナの天才ぶりに支えられたアルゼンチン
ベッケンバウアーの守備が支柱となったドイツ
ja.explanation: 1958年と1962年に連覇したブラジルが、いまだに連続でタイトルを防衛した最後の国です。

ko.question: 마지막으로 2회 연속 우승을 차지한 팀은 어디인가요?
ko.explanation: 1958년과 1962년 연속 우승을 거둔 브라질은 지금까지 타이틀 방어에 성공한 마지막 나라로 남아 있습니다.

pt-BR.question: Qual seleção foi a última a conquistar dois títulos consecutivos?
options: Itália, comandada por Meazza e Piola
Brasil, com Pelé e Garrincha no auge
Argentina, sob a genialidade de Maradona
Alemanha, apoiada na defesa de Beckenbauer
pt-BR.explanation: As conquistas consecutivas do Brasil em 1958 e 1962 seguem sendo a última vez que uma seleção conseguiu defender o título.

zh-Hans.question: 哪支球队是最后一支连续两次夺冠的队伍？
options: 由梅阿查和皮奥拉领衔的意大利
拥有巅峰期贝利和加林查的巴西
在马拉多纳天才发挥下的阿根廷
以贝肯鲍尔防线为支柱的德国
zh-Hans.explanation: 巴西在1958年和1962年连续夺冠，至今仍是任何国家最后一次成功卫冕。

questions.question: Which nation won back-to-back titles in 1934 and 1938?
questions.explanation: Italy won on home soil in 1934 and retained the title in France in 1938 — the first nation to win back-to-back.

questions.question: In which year did England win its only title?
questions.explanation: England won on home soil in 1966, beating West Germany 4–2 after extra time.

questions.question: Which nation is the only one to have competed in every single edition?
questions.explanation: Brazil has appeared in every tournament since 1930 — the only nation with a perfect attendance record.

questions.question: In 2014, Costa Rica topped a group containing Italy, England, and which other former champion?
questions.explanation: Costa Rica won Group D ahead of Uruguay, Italy, and England — all former champions — one of the biggest group-stage shocks ever.

questions.question: What is the highest-scoring match in tournament history, with 12 goals?
questions.explanation: Austria beat Switzerland 7–5 in a 1954 quarter-final, producing 12 goals — still the all-time record for a single match.

questions.question: Hard Rock Stadium, a 2026 venue in Miami, is home to which NFL team?
questions.explanation: Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens is home to the Miami Dolphins and has hosted multiple Super Bowls.

questions.question: How many titles has Brazil won?
questions.explanation: Brazil's 5 titles are the most of any nation — and they're the only team to play in every edition.

questions.question: The controversial 'Jabulani' was the official match ball for which edition?
questions.explanation: The Jabulani, used at the 2010 tournament, was criticised by goalkeepers and players for its unpredictable flight path.

questions.question: Which nation won its first title in 2010?
questions.explanation: Spain beat the Netherlands 1–0 in extra time for their first-ever title.

ar.question: أي منتخب فاز بلقبه الأول عام 2010؟
ar.explanation: تغلبت إسبانيا على هولندا 1–0 في الوقت الإضافي لتحرز لقبها الأول على الإطلاق.

de.question: Welche Nation gewann 2010 ihren ersten Titel?
de.explanation: Spanien besiegte die Niederlande in der Verlängerung mit 1:0 und holte den allerersten Titel.

es.question: ¿Qué selección ganó su primer título en 2010?
es.explanation: España venció a Países Bajos 1–0 en el tiempo extra para conseguir su primer título.

es-ES.question: ¿Qué selección ganó su primer título en 2010?
es-ES.explanation: España derrotó a Países Bajos por 1–0 en la prórroga para lograr su primer título.

fr.question: Quelle nation a remporté son premier titre en 2010 ?
fr.explanation: L'Espagne a battu les Pays-Bas 1–0 après prolongation pour décrocher son tout premier titre.

it.question: Quale nazionale ha vinto il suo primo titolo nel 2010?
it.explanation: La Spagna ha battuto i Paesi Bassi 1–0 ai supplementari conquistando il suo primo titolo in assoluto.

ja.question: 2010年に初優勝した国はどこでしょう？
ja.explanation: スペインは延長戦でオランダを1–0で破り、初のタイトルを獲得しました。

ko.question: 2010년에 첫 우승을 차지한 나라는 어디일까요?
ko.explanation: 스페인은 연장전에서 네덜란드를 1–0으로 꺾고 사상 첫 우승을 달성했습니다.

pt-BR.question: Qual seleção conquistou seu primeiro título em 2010?
pt-BR.explanation: A Espanha venceu os Países Baixos por 1 a 0 na prorrogação e conquistou seu primeiro título.

zh-Hans.question: 哪个国家在2010年赢得了首个冠军？
zh-Hans.explanation: 西班牙在加时赛中以1–0战胜荷兰，夺得队史首个冠军。

questions.question: In which edition was VAR (Video Assistant Referee) first used?
questions.explanation: VAR made its tournament debut in Russia in 2018, reviewing goals, penalties, red cards, and mistaken identity.

questions.question: What does VAR stand for?
questions.explanation: VAR helps match officials review decisions on goals, penalties, red cards, and mistaken identity using video footage.

ar.question: ماذا يعني اختصار VAR؟
ar.explanation: يساعد حكم الفيديو المساعد (VAR) طاقم المباراة على مراجعة القرارات المتعلقة بالأهداف وركلات الجزاء والبطاقات الحمراء والخطأ في تحديد هوية اللاعب باستخدام لقطات الفيديو.

de.question: Wofür steht VAR?
de.explanation: Der Videoschiedsrichter (VAR) unterstützt die Spieloffiziellen dabei, Entscheidungen zu Toren, Strafstößen, roten Karten und Spielerverwechslungen anhand von Videoaufnahmen zu überprüfen.

es.question: ¿Qué significa VAR?
es.explanation: El VAR (Árbitro Asistente de Video) ayuda a los árbitros del partido a revisar decisiones sobre goles, penales, tarjetas rojas e identidad equivocada usando imágenes de video.

es-ES.question: ¿Qué significa VAR?
es-ES.explanation: El VAR (Árbitro Asistente de Vídeo) ayuda a los colegiados a revisar decisiones sobre goles, penaltis, tarjetas rojas e identidad equivocada mediante imágenes de vídeo.

fr.question: Que signifie VAR ?
options: Very Angry Referee (Arbitre très en colère)
fr.explanation: L'arbitrage vidéo (VAR) aide les officiels du match à réexaminer les décisions concernant les buts, les penalties, les cartons rouges et les erreurs d'identité à l'aide d'images vidéo.

it.question: Cosa significa VAR?
options: Visual Assistance Replay (Replay di assistenza visiva)
it.explanation: Il VAR (arbitro assistente video) aiuta gli ufficiali di gara a rivedere le decisioni su gol, rigori, cartellini rossi ed errori di identità grazie alle immagini video.

ja.question: VARとは何の略ですか？
options: とても怒っている主審（Very Angry Referee）
ビデオ・アシスタント・レフェリー（Video Assistant Referee）
ビデオ分析レビュー（Video Analysis Review）
視覚補助リプレー（Visual Assistance Replay）
ja.explanation: VAR（ビデオ・アシスタント・レフェリー）は、映像を用いてゴール、PK、レッドカード、人違いに関する判定を試合の審判団が見直すのを支援します。

ko.question: VAR는 무엇의 약자인가요?
ko.explanation: VAR(비디오 보조 심판)는 영상 자료를 활용해 득점, 페널티킥, 레드카드, 선수 오인 판정에 대한 결정을 경기 심판진이 재검토하도록 돕습니다.

pt-BR.question: O que significa VAR?
pt-BR.explanation: O VAR (Árbitro Assistente de Vídeo) ajuda os árbitros da partida a revisar decisões sobre gols, pênaltis, cartões vermelhos e identidade equivocada usando imagens de vídeo.

zh-Hans.question: VAR 是什么的缩写？
options: 非常愤怒的裁判（Very Angry Referee）
视频助理裁判（Video Assistant Referee）
视频分析回看（Video Analysis Review）
视觉辅助回放（Visual Assistance Replay）
zh-Hans.explanation: VAR（视频助理裁判）通过视频画面帮助比赛裁判组对进球、点球、红牌以及认错球员等判罚进行复核。

questions.question: Uruguay won two titles. In which years?
questions.explanation: Uruguay won the inaugural tournament in 1930 on home soil and again in 1950 with the famous 'Maracanazo' in Brazil.

questions.question: What is the approximate seating capacity of MetLife Stadium, host of the 2026 final?
questions.explanation: MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey seats approximately 82,500. For comparison, the largest venue in tournament history was the Maracanã for the 1950 final, which held an estimated 199,854 spectators. The smallest venue ever used was the Pochtenoy Stadion in Switzerland (1954) with just 10,000 seats.

ar.question: ما هي السعة التقريبية لمقاعد ملعب ميت لايف، المستضيف للنهائي عام 2026؟
ar.explanation: يتسع ملعب ميت لايف في إيست رذرفورد بولاية نيو جيرسي لنحو 82,500 متفرج. وللمقارنة، كان أكبر ملعب في تاريخ البطولة هو ملعب ماراكانا في نهائي عام 1950، الذي استوعب ما يُقدَّر بـ 199,854 متفرجًا. أما أصغر ملعب استُخدم على الإطلاق فكان ملعب بوختنوي في سويسرا (1954) بسعة 10,000 مقعد فقط.

de.question: Wie hoch ist die ungefähre Zuschauerkapazität des MetLife Stadium, in dem das Finale 2026 ausgetragen wird?
de.explanation: Das MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, bietet rund 82.500 Zuschauern Platz. Zum Vergleich: Der größte Austragungsort in der Turniergeschichte war das Maracanã beim Finale 1950, das schätzungsweise 199.854 Zuschauer fasste. Der kleinste jemals genutzte Spielort war das Pochtenoy Stadion in der Schweiz (1954) mit gerade einmal 10.000 Plätzen.

es.question: ¿Cuál es la capacidad aproximada de asientos del MetLife Stadium, sede de la final de 2026?
es.explanation: El MetLife Stadium en East Rutherford, Nueva Jersey, tiene capacidad para unos 82,500 espectadores. Para comparar, el recinto más grande en la historia del torneo fue el Maracaná en la final de 1950, que albergó a unos 199,854 espectadores. El recinto más pequeño jamás utilizado fue el Pochtenoy Stadion en Suiza (1954), con apenas 10,000 asientos.

es-ES.question: ¿Cuál es la capacidad aproximada de asientos del MetLife Stadium, sede de la final de 2026?
es-ES.explanation: El MetLife Stadium de East Rutherford, Nueva Jersey, tiene capacidad para unos 82.500 espectadores. A modo de comparación, el recinto más grande de la historia del torneo fue el Maracaná en la final de 1950, que acogió a unos 199.854 espectadores. El recinto más pequeño que se ha utilizado nunca fue el Pochtenoy Stadion de Suiza (1954), con apenas 10.000 asientos.

fr.question: Quelle est la capacité d'accueil approximative du MetLife Stadium, hôte de la finale 2026 ?
fr.explanation: Le MetLife Stadium d'East Rutherford, dans le New Jersey, peut accueillir environ 82 500 spectateurs. À titre de comparaison, la plus grande enceinte de l'histoire du tournoi fut le Maracanã lors de la finale de 1950, qui accueillit environ 199 854 spectateurs. Le plus petit stade jamais utilisé fut le Pochtenoy Stadion en Suisse (1954), avec seulement 10 000 places.

it.question: Qual è la capienza approssimativa del MetLife Stadium, sede della finale del 2026?
it.explanation: Il MetLife Stadium di East Rutherford, nel New Jersey, può ospitare circa 82.500 spettatori. Per fare un confronto, l'impianto più grande nella storia del torneo fu il Maracanã per la finale del 1950, che accolse circa 199.854 spettatori. L'impianto più piccolo mai utilizzato fu il Pochtenoy Stadion in Svizzera (1954), con appena 10.000 posti.

ja.question: 2026年の決勝が開催されるメットライフ・スタジアムのおおよその収容人数はどれくらいですか？
ja.explanation: ニュージャージー州イーストラザフォードにあるメットライフ・スタジアムの収容人数は約82,500人です。比較すると、大会史上最大の会場は1950年の決勝が行われたマラカナンで、推定199,854人を収容しました。これまでに使用された最小の会場はスイスのポクテノイ・スタジオン（1954年）で、わずか10,000席でした。

ko.question: 2026년 결승전이 열리는 메트라이프 스타디움의 대략적인 수용 인원은 얼마입니까?
ko.explanation: 뉴저지주 이스트러더퍼드에 위치한 메트라이프 스타디움의 수용 인원은 약 82,500명입니다. 비교하자면, 대회 역사상 가장 큰 경기장은 1950년 결승전이 열린 마라카낭으로, 약 199,854명의 관중을 수용했습니다. 지금까지 사용된 가장 작은 경기장은 스위스의 포크테노이 슈타디온(1954년)으로 단 10,000석에 불과했습니다.

pt-BR.question: Qual é a capacidade aproximada de assentos do MetLife Stadium, sede da final de 2026?
pt-BR.explanation: O MetLife Stadium, em East Rutherford, Nova Jersey, comporta cerca de 82.500 espectadores. Para comparação, o maior estádio da história do torneio foi o Maracanã, na final de 1950, que recebeu cerca de 199.854 espectadores. O menor estádio já utilizado foi o Pochtenoy Stadion, na Suíça (1954), com apenas 10.000 lugares.

zh-Hans.question: 承办2026年决赛的大都会人寿球场大约能容纳多少观众？
zh-Hans.explanation: 位于新泽西州东拉瑟福德的大都会人寿球场可容纳约82,500名观众。相比之下，赛事史上最大的场馆是举办1950年决赛的马拉卡纳球场，估计容纳了199,854名观众。而有史以来使用过的最小场馆是瑞士的波克特诺伊球场（1954年），仅有10,000个座位。

questions.question: Germany famously thrashed Brazil 7–1 in a 2014 semi-final. Where was it played?
questions.explanation: The shock 7–1 result came in Belo Horizonte — one of the most stunning scorelines in the sport's history.

questions.question: Who is the youngest player to score in a tournament final?
questions.explanation: Pelé was just 17 when he scored twice in the 1958 final against Sweden. The next youngest was Kylian Mbappé, who was 19 when he scored in the 2018 final.

questions.question: Which legendary Hungarian scored 84 goals in 85 internationals and starred in the 1954 tournament?
questions.explanation: Puskás was the star of Hungary's 'Golden Team' that reached the 1954 final, though they lost to West Germany despite being heavy favorites.

questions.question: The 2006 'Battle of Nuremberg' (Portugal vs Netherlands) holds the record for most cards in a match. How many?
questions.explanation: Referee Valentin Ivanov showed 16 yellow cards and 4 red cards — 20 total — in one of the most ill-tempered matches ever.

questions.question: The current trophy is made of 18-carat gold. How much does it weigh?
questions.explanation: The trophy weighs 6.175 kg and has been awarded since 1974 after Brazil kept the original Jules Rimet Trophy permanently. Winners get to hold the real trophy only briefly on the pitch — they then receive a gold-plated bronze replica to keep. The original stays with the governing body and no nation can keep it permanently again.

questions.question: Who was the first player to score at five consecutive editions of the tournament?
questions.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo scored at every edition from 2006 to 2022 — five consecutive tournaments, a record no player had achieved before. The next longest streak is four consecutive editions, held by Miroslav Klose (2002–2014) and Pelé (1958–1970).

questions.question: Which legendary goalkeeper is the only keeper ever to win the Ballon d'Or?
questions.explanation: Soviet keeper Lev Yashin, the 'Black Spider,' won the Ballon d'Or in 1963 — still the only goalkeeper ever awarded it.

questions.question: All three 2026 host nations belong to which football confederation?
questions.explanation: The USA, Canada, and Mexico are all members of CONCACAF — the Confederation of North, Central American and Caribbean Association Football.

questions.question: Which edition holds the record for most goals per game (5.38)?
questions.explanation: The 1954 tournament averaged 5.38 goals per game across 26 matches — a record that will almost certainly never be broken.

questions.question: How many titles has Germany won?
questions.explanation: Germany has won four titles: 1954, 1974, 1990, and 2014.

questions.question: Which African nation stunned defending champions France in the 2002 opening match?
questions.explanation: Senegal beat France 1–0 in the tournament opener — one of the biggest upsets in the competition's history.

questions.question: Who scored the first hat-trick in a tournament final, in 1966?
questions.explanation: Geoff Hurst scored three for England against West Germany — a feat unmatched for 56 years until Mbappé's hat-trick in 2022.

questions.question: How many of the 48 teams reach the Round of 32 in 2026?
questions.explanation: The top two from each of the 12 groups (24) plus the 8 best third-placed teams advance — 32 in all.

questions.question: What was the score when Brazil beat Italy in the iconic 1970 final?
questions.explanation: Brazil's 4–1 victory in Mexico City is widely regarded as one of the greatest finals ever played.

questions.question: Panini has produced collectible sticker albums for the tournament since which year?
questions.explanation: Italian company Panini first published a tournament sticker album for the 1970 edition in Mexico, starting a global collecting tradition.

questions.question: Who is the oldest player to appear at the tournament?
questions.explanation: Egypt's Essam El Hadary was 45 years and 161 days old when he played against Saudi Arabia at the 2018 tournament.

questions.question: Estadio Azteca in Mexico City has hosted how many previous tournament finals?
questions.explanation: The Azteca hosted finals in both 1970 (Brazil 4–1 Italy) and 1986 (Argentina 3–2 West Germany).

questions.question: Against which team did Cristiano Ronaldo score a hat-trick in the 2018 group stage?
questions.explanation: Ronaldo's hat-trick in a thrilling 3–3 draw with Spain included a stunning late free kick on the opening day of 2018.

questions.question: How many teams compete in the 2026 edition?
questions.explanation: 2026 expanded the field from 32 to 48 nations, making it the largest edition ever.

questions.question: The Rose Bowl in Pasadena hosted which historic tournament final?
questions.explanation: The 1994 final at the Rose Bowl saw Brazil beat Italy on penalties — the first final decided by a shootout.

questions.question: How many tournament goals did Germany's Gerd Müller score in just two editions?
questions.explanation: Müller scored 10 goals in 1970 and 4 in 1974 — 14 in just 13 matches, a stunning strike rate.

questions.question: Which Brazilian appeared in three consecutive finals (1994, 1998, 2002)?
questions.explanation: Right-back Cafu is the only player to appear in three successive finals, winning two (1994 and 2002) and losing one (1998 to France). He captained Brazil in 2002 and earned 142 caps across his career — a Brazilian record at the time. Known for his tireless runs down the right flank, he was nicknamed 'Il Pendolino' (the express train) during his time at AC Milan.

questions.question: In which year did the USA achieve their best-ever finish, reaching the semi-finals?
questions.explanation: The USA reached the semi-finals at the inaugural 1930 tournament, losing 6–1 to Argentina.

questions.question: Who won the Golden Boot (top scorer) at the 2022 tournament?
questions.explanation: Mbappé scored 8 goals including a hat-trick in the final, edging out Messi on assists.

questions.question: Which Colombian star won the Golden Boot in 2014 with 6 goals?
questions.explanation: James Rodríguez dazzled in 2014, including a stunning volley against Uruguay voted the tournament's best goal.

questions.question: Which surprise team knocked out Italy in the group stage of the 1966 tournament?
questions.explanation: North Korea stunned Italy 1–0, one of the biggest upsets in history, before losing 5–3 to Portugal in the quarter-finals.

questions.question: 2026 is the first edition with 48 teams. How many competed in the previous format?
questions.explanation: The tournament expanded from 32 to 48 teams for 2026, adding 16 more nations and an extra knockout round.

questions.question: Where was the very first edition held, in 1930?
questions.explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the inaugural 1930 tournament on home soil.

questions.question: The 'Maracanazo' shocked Brazil in 1950. Who beat them in the deciding match?
questions.explanation: Uruguay beat Brazil 2–1 in the 1950 final group match at the Maracanã, silencing nearly 200,000 fans.

questions.question: Which nation has appeared in the most finals?
questions.explanation: Germany has reached the final 8 times (1954, 1966, 1974, 1982, 1986, 1990, 2002, 2014), winning four. Next is Brazil with 7 finals and 5 wins — the most titles of any nation. Italy and Argentina follow with 6 final appearances each (Italy won 4, Argentina won 3).

questions.question: Two yellow cards in the same match result in…
options: A free subscription to the bench
questions.explanation: A second yellow in one match becomes a red card, and the player misses the next match.

questions.question: Who won the Golden Boot in 2010 with 5 goals and 3 assists?
questions.explanation: Germany's Thomas Müller won the Golden Boot at age 20 in his first major tournament.

questions.question: Which Canadian 2026 venue features a retractable roof?
questions.explanation: BC Place in Vancouver features a retractable roof, ensuring matches can be played regardless of weather.

questions.question: Which was the first Asian country to reach the semi-finals?
questions.explanation: South Korea reached the semi-finals as co-hosts in 2002, defeating Spain and Italy along the way.

questions.question: Who won the Golden Ball (best player) at the 2010 tournament?
questions.explanation: Uruguay's Diego Forlán won the Golden Ball in 2010, scoring 5 goals as Uruguay reached the semi-finals.

questions.question: 'La Ola' (the Mexican wave) became a global phenomenon at which tournament?
questions.explanation: While the wave existed before, it gained worldwide fame during the 1986 tournament in Mexico, earning the name 'Mexican wave.'

questions.question: Which player won three titles — the only one to do so?
questions.explanation: Pelé won with Brazil in 1958, 1962 and 1970 — still the only player with three winners' medals.

questions.question: What unusual format did the 1950 tournament use instead of a knockout final?
questions.explanation: The 1950 tournament had a round-robin final group instead of a single knockout final — the only time this format was used.

questions.question: Who is the all-time leading goal scorer across all tournaments?
questions.explanation: Lionel Messi overtook Miroslav Klose's longstanding record of 16 goals during the 2026 tournament, becoming the all-time leading scorer.

questions.question: The iconic 'Tango' ball design, with its distinctive pattern of interlocking triads, was introduced at which tournament?
questions.explanation: The Adidas Tango debuted at the 1978 tournament in Argentina and its design influenced every match ball until 2006.

questions.question: Which captain lifted the trophy for England in 1966?
questions.explanation: Bobby Moore captained England to their only title in 1966, widely regarded as one of the greatest defenders ever.

questions.question: In which year was the tournament first broadcast on television?
questions.explanation: The 1954 tournament in Switzerland was the first to be televised, bringing the event to living rooms across Europe.

questions.question: The original trophy was stolen in England in 1966 and famously found by…
questions.explanation: The Jules Rimet Trophy was found by a dog named Pickles, who discovered it wrapped in newspaper under a hedge.

questions.question: For how many editions (1998–2022) did the tournament use a 32-team format?
questions.explanation: The 32-team format ran for seven editions from France 1998 through Qatar 2022 before expanding to 48 teams.

questions.question: The 'Miracle of Bern' describes which team's shock 1954 final victory?
questions.explanation: West Germany stunned mighty Hungary 3–2 in the 1954 final despite trailing 2–0 early on.

questions.question: Who is the oldest player to score at the tournament, netting at 42 years old?
questions.explanation: Cameroon's Roger Milla scored against Russia at the 1994 tournament at 42 years and 39 days old.

questions.question: Brazil was awarded permanent possession of the original Jules Rimet Trophy after winning their…
questions.explanation: After winning their third title in 1970, Brazil kept the Jules Rimet Trophy permanently, and a new trophy was created for 1974.

questions.question: In which year was the first penalty shootout held at the tournament?
questions.explanation: The first shootout came in 1982 when West Germany beat France in the semi-final after a 3–3 draw.

questions.question: 2026 marks Mexico's third time as host. When were the first two?
questions.explanation: Mexico hosted in 1970 (won by Brazil) and 1986 (won by Argentina), making 2026 their record third time.

questions.question: Which team scored the most goals in a single match — 10?
questions.explanation: Hungary scored 10 times in their 10–1 thrashing of El Salvador in 1982 — the most by one team in a single match.

questions.question: What is the smallest nation by population to qualify for the tournament?
questions.explanation: Iceland, with a population of around 350,000, qualified for the 2018 tournament and drew 1–1 with Argentina in the group stage.

questions.question: Who were the champions in 2022?
questions.explanation: Argentina beat France on penalties after a 3–3 final — their third title and Lionel Messi's first.

questions.question: Before Messi surpassed him, who held the record for most tournament appearances?
questions.explanation: Germany's Lothar Matthäus played 25 matches across five editions (1982–1998) before Messi overtook him in 2022.

questions.question: Who scored a hat-trick in the 2022 final — and still finished on the losing side?
questions.explanation: Kylian Mbappé scored all three of France's goals, but Argentina won the shootout.

questions.question: Which African nation shocked the world by reaching the quarter-finals in 1990?
questions.explanation: Cameroon beat reigning champions Argentina in the opening match and reached the quarter-finals, led by 38-year-old Roger Milla.

questions.question: Who has played the most matches in the tournament's history?
questions.explanation: Lionel Messi passed Lothar Matthäus in 2022, reaching 26 career appearances.

questions.question: Who holds the record for most goals in a single edition (13)?
questions.explanation: France's Just Fontaine scored 13 goals at the 1958 tournament — a record that still stands.

questions.question: At kickoff, the ball can be played in which direction?
options: To a teammate in the other half
questions.explanation: Since 2016, the ball can be kicked in any direction at kickoff. Previously it had to move forward.

questions.question: Which nation has won the most titles?
questions.explanation: Brazil has lifted the trophy 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994 and 2002) — more than any other nation.

questions.question: In 2018, a penalty was awarded via VAR review for the first time in a final. Which team benefited?
questions.explanation: France were awarded a penalty after VAR confirmed a handball by Croatia's Ivan Perišić — Antoine Griezmann converted to make it 2–1.

questions.question: Which Portuguese legend scored 9 goals at the 1966 tournament?
questions.explanation: Eusébio's 9 goals made him the top scorer in 1966, leading Portugal to third place on their debut.

questions.question: What determines how much stoppage time is added at the end of each half?
options: The referee's judgment based on stoppages
questions.explanation: The referee adds time for substitutions, injuries, time-wasting, VAR reviews, and other stoppages — there's no fixed amount.

questions.question: The 2022 edition in Qatar was unique for being held in which months?
questions.explanation: Qatar 2022 was moved to November–December to avoid the extreme summer heat — the first winter edition in the Northern Hemisphere.

questions.question: Which national team is known as 'Les Bleus'?
questions.explanation: France's national team is nicknamed 'Les Bleus' (The Blues) after the colour of their home jerseys.

questions.question: Who won the Golden Boot at the 1986 tournament with 6 goals?
questions.explanation: England's Gary Lineker scored 6 goals, including a hat-trick against Poland, to win the 1986 Golden Boot.

questions.question: How many cities in the United States host matches in 2026?
questions.explanation: Eleven US cities host matches, from New York/New Jersey and Los Angeles to Dallas, Houston, Atlanta, Miami, Seattle, Philadelphia, Kansas City, the San Francisco Bay Area, and Boston.

questions.question: How many Canadian cities host matches in 2026?
questions.explanation: Canada hosts matches in two cities — Vancouver (BC Place) and Toronto (BMO Field).

questions.question: How many titles has Argentina won?
questions.explanation: Argentina has won three titles: 1978, 1986, and most recently 2022 in Qatar.

questions.question: AT&T Stadium, a 2026 venue near Dallas, is the home of which NFL team?
questions.explanation: AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas is home to the Dallas Cowboys and can seat over 80,000 for football.

questions.question: How far is the penalty spot from the goal line?
questions.explanation: The penalty spot is 12 yards (approximately 11 metres) from the centre of the goal line.

questions.question: How long is a standard match, excluding stoppage time?
questions.explanation: A match is played in two 45-minute halves. Extra time in knockout rounds adds two more 15-minute periods.

questions.question: How many Mexican cities host matches in 2026?
questions.explanation: Mexico hosts matches in three cities — Mexico City (Estadio Azteca), Guadalajara (Estadio Akron), and Monterrey (Estadio BBVA).

questions.question: How many tournament goals did Brazil's Ronaldo score across his career?
questions.explanation: Ronaldo scored 15 goals across 1998, 2002, and 2006 — including both goals in the 2002 final.

questions.question: How many substitutions does each team get per match in 2026?
questions.explanation: Each team may make up to 5 substitutions, using a maximum of 3 stoppages in play plus half-time and extra-time breaks.

questions.question: Can a goal be scored directly from a goal kick?
options: No, another player must touch it first
Yes, but only against the opposing team
questions.explanation: A goal can be scored directly from a goal kick, but only against the opposing team — an own goal from a goal kick results in a corner.

questions.question: What happens if a player collects two yellow cards across two different group-stage matches?
options: They are suspended for the next match
questions.explanation: Two yellows in separate matches leads to a one-match suspension, which is why players on a yellow are sometimes rested.

questions.question: Who is the youngest player to appear at the tournament?
questions.explanation: Northern Ireland's Norman Whiteside was 17 years and 41 days old when he played against Yugoslavia at the 1982 tournament.

questions.question: How many matches will be played at the 2026 tournament?
questions.explanation: The 48-team format means 104 matches — up from 64 in the previous 32-team era.

questions.question: What is the fastest red card in tournament history?
questions.explanation: José Batista of Uruguay was sent off after just 56 seconds against Scotland in 1986 — the fastest dismissal ever.

questions.question: Where will the 2026 final be played?
questions.explanation: The final is set for MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, just outside New York City.

questions.question: How many titles has Italy won?
questions.explanation: Italy has won four titles (1934, 1938, 1982, 2006), tied with Germany for the second-most.

questions.question: Which country hosted the tournament in 1994?
questions.explanation: The USA hosted the 1994 edition, which set all-time attendance records that stood for decades.

questions.question: In football, what does it mean when a player scores a "brace"?
options: They score two goals in the same match
They score three goals in the same match
They score and then brace themselves for the inevitable VAR review
They score in two consecutive matches
questions.explanation: A brace refers to a player scoring two goals in a single match. Scoring three goals is called a hat-trick.

questions.question: In football, what does it mean when a player earns a 'cap' for their national team?
options: They receive a commemorative hat after scoring their first goal
They are selected and play in an official match for their country
They are named captain of their national team
They win an individual award at the tournament
questions.explanation: A 'cap' represents one official appearance for a player's national team. The term originated from the practice of awarding players a physical cap for each match they played. When commentators say a player has 'X caps,' they mean that player has appeared in X official international matches for their country.

questions.question: What is the Golden Boot award given for at the tournament?
questions.explanation: The Golden Boot goes to the tournament's top scorer. If players are tied on goals, assists are the tiebreaker, followed by fewer minutes played. Past winners include Harry Kane (2018, 6 goals) and Kylian Mbappé (2022, 8 goals).

questions.question: In football, what does a 'clean sheet' mean?
options: A team wins by scoring 3 or more goals
A team completes a match without conceding any goals
A player makes a perfect pass without any errors
A goalkeeper makes a save without using their hands
questions.explanation: A clean sheet refers to a defensive achievement where a team prevents the opposing team from scoring throughout the entire match. It's a key metric for measuring defensive performance in football.

questions.question: Brazil is the only nation to have appeared at every edition of the tournament.
questions.explanation: Brazil has played in every edition since 1930 — the only nation to have never missed one. They've won five titles along the way. Germany and Italy are second and third respectively in terms of tournament appearances, with Germany appearing in 19 editions and Italy in 18 editions.

questions.question: The 2026 tournament will be the first to feature 48 teams.
questions.explanation: The 2026 edition expands from 32 to 48 teams, meaning 104 matches across 16 host cities in the United States, Mexico, and Canada.

questions.question: VAR (Video Assistant Referee) was first used at the 2014 tournament in Brazil.
questions.explanation: VAR debuted at the 2018 tournament in Russia. The 2014 edition in Brazil still relied entirely on on-field officials.

questions.question: Germany has won the tournament more times than Italy.
questions.explanation: They're tied — both nations have won four titles. Germany won in 1954, 1974, 1990, and 2014; Italy in 1934, 1938, 1982, and 2006.

questions.question: Miroslav Klose holds the all-time scoring record with 16 goals across four editions.
questions.explanation: Klose scored 16 goals for Germany across four editions (2002–2014), but Lionel Messi surpassed that record during the 2026 tournament.

questions.question: The 2022 final between Argentina and France was decided on penalties.
questions.explanation: After a 3–3 draw through extra time, Argentina beat France 4–2 in the penalty shootout. Many consider it the greatest final ever played.

questions.question: No host nation has ever been eliminated in the group stage.
questions.explanation: South Africa became the first (and so far only) host nation eliminated in the group stage, at their own 2010 tournament.

questions.question: France won their first title at the 1998 tournament, which they hosted in France.
questions.explanation: France hosted and won the 1998 tournament, defeating Brazil 3–0 in the final. They won their second title in 2018 in Russia.

questions.question: The fastest goal in tournament history was scored in under 15 seconds.
questions.explanation: Turkey's Hakan Şükür scored just 10.8 seconds into the 2002 third-place match against South Korea — the fastest goal in tournament history.

questions.question: The 2002 tournament was the first to be co-hosted by two countries.
questions.explanation: South Korea and Japan co-hosted in 2002, making it the first multi-nation edition. The 2026 tournament goes further with three co-hosts.

questions.question: Who is this legendary Brazilian forward?
questions.explanation: Pelé won three titles (1958, 1962, 1970) and scored 12 goals across four editions. Widely regarded as one of the greatest players ever.

questions.question: Who is this French midfielder and manager?
questions.explanation: Zinedine Zidane led France to their first title in 1998, scoring twice in the final. His 2006 final headbutt on Materazzi became one of football's most infamous moments.

questions.question: Who is this Brazilian striker who scored 15 goals across four editions?
questions.explanation: Ronaldo Nazário scored 15 goals across four editions (1998–2010), winning the Golden Boot in 2002 with eight goals as Brazil took the title.

questions.question: Who is this Argentine forward who finally lifted the trophy in 2022?
questions.explanation: Lionel Messi led Argentina to victory in 2022 at his fifth and final edition. He scored seven goals including two in the final, winning the Golden Ball.

questions.question: Who is this Portuguese captain who has scored at five consecutive editions?
questions.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo scored at every edition from 2006 to 2022 — five consecutive tournaments. He holds the record for most international goals by any male player.

questions.question: Who is this French forward who scored a hat trick in the 2022 final?
questions.explanation: Kylian Mbappé scored a hat trick in the 2022 final and won the Golden Boot with eight goals. He first won the title as a teenager in 2018.

questions.question: Who is this Brazilian playmaker known for his dazzling skills and iconic smile?
questions.explanation: Ronaldinho was part of Brazil's 2002 winning squad and scored a memorable free kick against England in the quarterfinals. He won the Ballon d'Or in 2005.

questions.question: Who is this Croatian midfielder who led his country to the 2018 final?
questions.explanation: Luka Modrić captained Croatia to the 2018 final and won the Golden Ball as the tournament's best player. He also led them to third place in 2022.

questions.question: Who is this Brazilian forward playing at the 2026 tournament?
questions.explanation: Neymar is Brazil's all-time leading scorer and is competing at his third edition in 2026 after injuries limited his 2014 and 2022 campaigns.

questions.question: Who is this Ivorian striker, one of Africa's greatest players?
questions.explanation: Didier Drogba represented Ivory Coast at three editions (2006, 2010, 2014). He's credited with helping to broker a ceasefire in the Ivorian civil war through football.
